title: Session Inspector UX — Next Wave: Operating Picture, Agent Diff, Command Palette
description: Pre-fabricated lane prompts for the next wave of Session Inspector UX features. These launch after current 4 lanes complete.

lanes.title: Operating Picture — Live Mission Dashboard
lanes.description: Right-side panel showing: current goal, phase, file claims, active tool, risk level, tests last run, git status, blocked reason, next planned action. Core UX: 'I should never have to ask what the agent is doing.'
lanes.prompt:
Build an Operating Picture panel — a live mission dashboard for the desktop app at packages/app/src/components/inspector/operating-picture.tsx.

It should show:
- Current goal (from event stream session.next.step_started)
- Current phase (from lifecycle events)
- Current file claims (from coordination events)
- Current tool running (from tool.started events)
- Risk level (derived from state)
- Tests last run
- Git status
- Blocked reason, if blocked
- Next planned action

The data source should be the InspectorContext from Lane 2. Create sub-components: MissionStatus, ClaimsMap, FileClaimsRow. Use the SDK event stream for real-time updates.

Must be SolidJS with @opencode-ai/ui components. TypeScript.

lanes.title: Agent Diff Review — Explainable, Revertible Changes
lanes.description: Dedicated Agent Changes lens: grouped by file, by agent turn, by claim, by lifecycle phase. Shows why each file changed, whether agent read the file first, whether edit was direct/patch/generated/formatted. One-click revert per hunk/file/turn/phase. 'Explain this hunk' click-to-see-what-issue-it-fixes.
lanes.prompt:
Build an Agent Diff Review view at packages/app/src/components/inspector/agent-diff.tsx.

Features:
- Group diffs by: file, agent turn, claim, lifecycle phase
- Each hunk shows: status icon, before/after, why it changed, which tool wrote it, what tests cover it, what risk remains
- One-click revert per hunk, file, turn, or phase
- 'Explain this hunk' button that queries the event ledger for context (what issue, which prompt, what evidence)
- Show whether agent read the file first before editing
- Show edit type: direct, patch, generated by tool, or auto-formatted

The data comes from file.edited events in the InspectorContext, correlated with tool call events. Use SolidJS. Wire a revert action through the SDK. TypeScript strict.

lanes.title: Safe Stop / Resume / Checkpoint Timeline
lanes.description: Big obvious 'Stop Safely' control. Stops new tool calls, lets current mutation finish/rollback, persists state, writes checkpoint summary. Shows: 'Stopped after editing 2 files. No tests run yet. Resume from validation phase?' Checkpoint timeline: Baseline → Plan accepted → Files claimed → Patch applied → Tests failed → Repair applied → Tests passed → Checkpoint created → PR ready. Each checkpoint: diff summary, test summary, restore button, commit SHA.
lanes.prompt:
Build Safe Stop/Resume controls and Checkpoint Timeline at packages/app/src/components/inspector/.

Stop Button:
- Big obvious 'Stop Safely' control in toolbar
- On click: stop new tool calls via SDK, let current mutation finish, persist event state, write checkpoint
- After stop shows summary panel: what changed, what didn't finish, resume/restart buttons

Checkpoint Timeline:
- Shows timeline nodes: Baseline, Plan accepted, Files claimed, Patch applied, Tests failed, Tests passed, Checkpoint created, PR ready
- Each node: diff summary, test summary, restore button, export button
- Click a checkpoint to restore that state

The timeline is derived from the event ledger (checkpoint.created events, phase transitions). Use events from InspectorContext. SolidJS components.

lanes.title: Command Palette — Keyboard-First IDE Surface
lanes.description: Universal command palette (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K). Commands include: Run tests for changed files, Explain current diff, Revert last agent edit, Checkpoint now, Show file claims, Show failed tools, Open event timeline, Ask agent to continue from selected error, Switch model, Make plan only, Apply patch only, Stop after next tool, Export debug packet, Generate PR description. Works everywhere, keyboard-first, context-aware based on selection.
lanes.prompt:
Build a universal Command Palette at packages/app/src/components/inspector/command-palette.tsx.

Trigger: Cmd+K / Ctrl+K globally.

Commands (at minimum):
- Run tests for changed files
- Explain current diff
- Revert last agent edit
- Checkpoint now
- Show file claims
- Show failed tools
- Open event timeline
- Ask agent to continue from selected error
- Switch model for next turn
- Make plan only
- Apply patch only
- Stop after next tool
- Export debug packet
- Summarize branch state
- Generate PR description

Features:
- Fuzzy search
- Keyboard navigation (arrows, enter, escape)
- Context-aware: if user selected text/file/event/error, commands scope to that selection
- Shows keyboard shortcut for each command
- Categories: Run, Inspect, Edit, Navigate, Export, Settings

Implement as a SolidJS portal overlay. Use the existing command infrastructure (packages/app/src/context/command.tsx). TypeScript strict. The commands should call the SDK or existing backend APIs.

lanes.title: Touch Set Preview + Claims Map
lanes.description: Before agent edits, show planned files to read, modify, protect. Show likelihood of tests needed, risk level, reason for each file. Allow: approve all, read-only mode, deny path, add path claim. Live repo tree showing files as: unclaimed, claimed, read-only, protected, stale claim, conflict, changed since claim. Click to see claim owner, time, digest, release button.
lanes.prompt:
Build Touch Set Preview and Claims Map at packages/app/src/components/inspector/touch-set-preview.tsx and claims-map.tsx.

Touch Set Preview:
- Shows planned: files to read, files to modify, files protected
- Shows per-file: reason for touching, risk level, associated tests
- Actions: Approve all, Approve selected, Read-only investigation, Deny path, Add path claim, Require tests before checkpoint
- Can expand each file to see proposed changes

Claims Map:
- Live repo tree with files color-coded: unclaimed (gray), claimed by current (blue), claimed by other (orange), read-only (pink), protected (red), stale claim (yellow), conflict (purple), changed since claim (green)
- Click file to show: claim owner, claim time, base digest, current digest, associated mission, Release Claim button

Use coordination events from InspectorContext. SolidJS. The touch set preview should show before agent mutations through the SDK's planning/claim events.

lanes.title: 'Why Did You Do That?' — Action Explanations
lanes.description: Every tool call, file edit, phase transition, permission request/refusal gets a 'Why?' button. Shows: what issue it fixes, which prompt/request caused it, which tool wrote it, what evidence supports it, what tests cover it, what risk remains. Grounded in the event ledger, not generated from vibes.
lanes.prompt:
Build the 'Why Did You Do That?' explanation system.

Backend (packages/opencode/src/event/explanation.ts):
- function explainEvent(eventId: string): Explanation
- Traces through the event ledger: finds parent events (the prompt/turn that caused it), sibling events (files read before), child events (tests run after)
- Returns structured explanation: { what, why_this_file, which_prompt, which_tool, evidence, test_coverage, risk }

Frontend (packages/app/src/components/inspector/):
- 'Why?' button next to every event in the timeline
- Click opens a panel with structured explanation
- Links to the original prompt, the tool call that made the edit, the test results
- Cached: don't recompute explanations

SolidJS for UI. Effect on backend. TypeScript.

lanes.title: Error Recovery Guided Workflow
lanes.description: When a tool fails, show not just 'Tool failed' but: what failed, why it likely failed (from DuckDB failure hotspots view), whether it's recoverable, safe next actions. Buttons: Open failing test, Ask agent to repair, Run only failing tests, Show last related edit, Revert suspected hunk, Mark as pre-existing, Escalate to full investigation.
lanes.prompt:
Build Error Recovery guided workflow at packages/app/src/components/inspector/error-recovery.tsx.

When a tool fails (listening to tool.failed events from InspectorContext):
- Show error card with: what failed, why (from DuckDB failure hotspots), recoverable flag, safe next actions
- Buttons: Open failing test, Ask agent to repair, Run only failing tests, Show last related edit, Revert suspected hunk, Mark as pre-existing, Escalate to full investigation
- Group errors by normalized error code (tool.timeout, lifecycle.phase_gate_denied, etc.)
- Show count of similar errors from this session

SolidJS. TypeScript. Use existing dialog infrastructure.

dependencies.launch_condition: All 4 current lanes complete and handoffs verified
